images.alt:
🎙️ Charaktere & Cast (Japanisch)
»Myōjin« von Kazuyuki Okitsu
Der Anführer des Teams Great Cluster und einer der absoluten Topspieler von Dark Machine. Sowohl seine Fähigkeiten als auch seine Popularität sind überwältigend. Dank seines umfangreichen Wissens über das Spiel zeigt er außerdem besonderes Interesse am Neuling Kairi. Privat ist er ein großer Fan von Nihonshu.

»Ryō« von Ryōta Suzuki
Der Anführer von Aldabil, einem Team unter dem siebten Senki Ajima. Sein Kampfstil ist aggressiv und voller Tempo. Wegen seines aufbrausenden und gewalttätigen Wesens wird er von Ajima häufig für Einschüchterungen und andere grobe Aufgaben eingesetzt.

»Kinji« von Genta Nakamura
Ein Mitglied des Teams Aldabil unter Ajima. Er ist seit seiner Kindheit mit Ryō und Hiru befreundet. Auch wenn sie sich oft streiten, verbindet die drei eine enge Freundschaft.

»Hiru« von Sōhei Horikane
Ebenfalls ein Mitglied von Aldabil und Kindheitsfreund von Ryō und Kinji. Trotz häufiger Reibereien halten die drei fest zusammen.

📜 Beschreibung 》
In einer nahen Zukunft, in der das E-Sport-Game DARK MACHINE die Gesellschaft beherrscht, zieht es Kairi in die Sonderzone Golden Valley – auf der Suche nach dem legendären Spieler Senki. Dort begegnet er dem geheimnisvollen Mädchen Rudora und wird selbst in die erbarmungslose Welt des Spiels hineingezogen. Während sein verborgenes Talent erwacht, beginnt für Kairi ein Kampf um Wahrheit, Macht und sein eigenes Schicksal.

images.alt:
Der Verlag Manga Cult bringt den Action-Manga »Grappler Baki« von Keisuke Itagaki nach Deutschland! Band 1 erscheint nach aktueller Planung voraussichtlich im Dezember 2026. Die Reihe wird als 3 in 1 veröffentlicht (insgesamt 14 Bände)

In Japan liefen die einzelnen Kapitel von Oktober 1991 bis Juni 1999 im »Weekly  Shounen Champion« Magazin von Akita Shoten und umfasst 371 Kapitel, die in der ursprünglich fassung in 42 Bände zusammengefasst wurden. Eine englische Übersetzung erscheint bei Kodama (14 Bände).

📜 Beschreibung (Manga Cult)》
Der 17-jährige Baki Hanma ist ein außergewöhnlicher Kämpfer, der seinesgleichen sucht. Mit unbeirrter Entschlossenheit stürzt er sich in die unbarmherzigen Arenen der illegalen Untergrundkämpfe – Orte, an denen Regeln keine Rolle spielen und jeder Kampf an die Grenze des Menschlichen geht. Obwohl er noch ein Teenager ist, stellt er sich gefährlichen Gegnern, die keinerlei Furcht kennen. Denn Bakis Antrieb beruht nicht nur auf brennendem Ehrgeiz, sondern vor allem auf einem unerbittlichen Ziel: stärker zu werden als sein verhasster Vater, die lebende Legende Yujiro Hanma! 

Bild
Baki The Grappler Kanzenban © KEISUKE ITAGAKI 2007 / Akita Publishing Co., Ltd.

record.bridgyOriginalText: So here’s how I used Claude Opus 4.6 on a recent writing project. In late 2022 I drafted an article on Global Economic Risks and Headwinds for Green Econometrics, a consulting firm. I published it on their SubStack in early 2023. I recently updated the original article to reflect current conditions, including the Iran War, Climate Change, as well as other geopolitic risks to the global economy and financial markets. During the update process I tasked Claude with being my research asst & image generator for new charts & graphs. To zero-in on the best research in just the areas I personally identified as high risk, I created a detailed prompt w/specific instructions re the kind of data I was after. Then I had the #AI create the charts & graphs I needed for visual data representations; and finally, I had Claude scrape the web for relevant quotes from experts, which I vetted then included. I fed the original article to Claude for final production. The work is very clearly mine and I claim all copyright to it.

external.description: SiFiveは、2015年にカリフォルニア大学バークレー校のエンジニアによって設立されたオープンソースのチップ設計企業です。 同社は、4億ドルを超える資金調達ラウンドを完了し、企業価値が36億5000万ドルと評価されました。 SiFiveのRISC-Vオープンチップ設計は、現在エヌビディアのGPUコンピューターシステムAI帝国を支えるIntelのx86やARMではなく、RISCプロセッサに基づいています。 今回のラウンドには、アトレイデス・マネジメントがリード投資家として参加し、エヌビディアも投資家として名を連ねました。 アトレイデス・マネジメントは、以前にもセレブラス・システムズの10億ドル規模の資金調達ラウンドにも投資しています。 アポロ・グローバル・マネジメントやT.ロウ・プライスなども今回のラウンドに参加しています。 SiFiveのビジネスモデルは、かつてのARMと同様に、チップ設計をライセンス供与し、顧客が自社のニーズに合わせて設計を修正することを可能にするもので、自社でチップを販売することはありません。 ARMは今年3月に、Metaと共同開発したAIチップを初めて製造しました。 SiFiveは、オープンで中立的なチップ設計を提供しており、特定の顧客に依存していません。 RISC-Vはこれまで、組み込みシステムなどの小規模な用途に適したチップとして知られていましたが、今回の資金調達とエヌビディアの注目により、AIデータセンター向けのCPUへの進出を目指しています。 SiFiveの設計は、エヌビディアのCUDAソフトウェアやNVLink Fusionと連携し、エヌビディアの「AIファクトリー」に様々なCPUを接続できるラックサーバーシステムを構築します。 ライバルであるIntelやAMDがエヌビディアのGPUと競合する中、エヌビディアはオープンで完全に代替的な技術に基づくCPUを設計できる11歳のスタートアップを支援しています。

images.alt:
"A blue jay bursts away from a small swing‑shaped feeder, caught in the instant of takeoff. Its wings are flung wide, each feather sharply defined — bands of electric blue, deep indigo, and crisp white flashing like stained glass in motion. The bird’s body tilts forward with purpose, legs tucked, tail fanned slightly for balance.

Clamped firmly in its beak is a whole peanut, the shell pale and textured against the jay’s sleek, vivid plumage. You can almost sense the determination in the bird’s posture — a quick snatch‑and‑go, a triumphant escape with its prize.

The wooden feeder beneath it is just beginning to fall out of focus, as if the bird has already left that moment behind. The background melts into a soft blur of muted greens and browns, making the blue jay stand out like a bright spark of color and motion. The whole scene feels alive, crisp, and full of energy — a split second of wild grace frozen in midair." - Microsoft Copilot

record.text: Red‑Bird at the Stone

images.alt:
"A male northern cardinal stands boldly in the grass, his body a brilliant, uninterrupted red that seems to glow against the green around him. His crest rises to a sharp point, giving him a look of alert confidence, as if he’s surveying his territory with quiet authority. The black mask around his face frames his eyes and surrounds his thick, cone‑shaped beak, creating a dramatic contrast that makes his expression feel almost sculpted.

The grass beneath him is a mix of soft greens and scattered small plants, textured and uneven, like a natural stage he’s chosen for this moment. In front of him lies a pale patch—perhaps a stone or a bit of debris—its light color only emphasizing the cardinal’s vivid plumage. The background stays gentle and unobtrusive, allowing the bird’s red feathers to dominate the scene with striking clarity.

The overall impression is one of presence and intensity: a flash of living color standing still just long enough to be admired." - Microsoft Copilot

Anti-data center vote in Wisconsin puts future AI projects on notice: Residents of Port Washington, Wisconsin, have done something no other community in the country has done with data centers: They've voted to put the brakes on future development in the region by approving a referendum. From now…

external.description:
In context: Tech companies have been racing to build the infrastructure needed to power AI, and massive data centers are taking shape around the US as a result. But the facilities are huge consumers of electricity and water, and some nearby communities have started to feel the squeeze. Their construction also generates noise around the clock. Pushback, then, was probably inevitable.
Residents of Port Washington, Wisconsin, have done something no other community in the country has done with data centers: They've voted to put the brakes on future development in the region by approving a referendum. From now on, city officials require voter approval before handing out tax incentives worth more than $10 million to developers. And they've done so with a pretty decisive margin, too. About 66% of voters backed the plan, according to unofficial county results.
This whole thing started because of a planned $15 billion AI campus in the area from Vantage Data Centers, which is being built in partnership with OpenAI and Oracle. That project is tied to the Trump administration's "Stargate" initiative, a broad push to build out AI infrastructure across the country. The campus broke ground back in December and is set to receive around $458 million in tax benefits from the city, according to The Hill. Naturally, not everyone was thrilled about that.
Those concerns eventually led to the formation of a local nonprofit called Great Lakes Neighbors United, which organized the opposition and collected over 1,000 signatures to get the measure on the ballot. Their grievances ranged from noise pollution and freshwater use to rising energy costs.
US senators have also probed whether AI data centers are driving up electricity costs for millions of consumers. In fact, one study found that prices surged by as much as 267% over five years in areas near significant data center activity.
The referendum won't actually stop the Vantage project that's already underway – it's aimed at whatever comes next. There's also a legal challenge looming. The Metropolitan Milwaukee Association of Commerce filed a lawsuit back in January, arguing that the measure violates state law, so this could get tied up in court pretty quickly.
That said, Port Washington isn't alone in pushing back, and it does set a precedent of sorts. Monterey Park, California, has a vote coming up in June on banning new data center construction entirely. Augusta Township in Michigan will decide in August whether to override a local ordinance that greenlit a similar project. And Janesville, Wisconsin, has its own vote scheduled for November.
The opposition isn't limited to local ballots, either. According to Data Center Watch, a tracking project from AI company 10a Labs, around 20 data center projects worth a combined $98 billion were blocked or delayed between March and June of last year alone.
external.title: Anti-data center vote in Wisconsin puts future AI projects on notice

record.text: Mozilla says Microsoft is using Copilot and Edge to tighten its grip on Windows: In a recent statement, Mozilla argued that Microsoft's design choices – particularly those that link the Windows experience tightly to Edge and Copilot – undermine genuine user control. When Microsoft embeds features…

external.description:
Editor's take: As Microsoft builds Copilot AI deeper into Windows, the move is rekindling debate over how much control any one company should exercise over the computing environment that so many people rely on. For Mozilla, that question has become a renewed challenge to Microsoft's dominance over the desktop and its implications for competition.
In a recent statement, Mozilla argued that Microsoft's design choices – particularly those that link the Windows experience tightly to Edge and Copilot – undermine genuine user control. When Microsoft embeds features that favor its own browser and AI tools, it removes opportunities for competing software to be used at all, Mozilla said.
At issue is not just how people interact with Microsoft's latest AI layer, but also how deeply the company's software ecosystem shapes user behavior. Changing a default browser in Windows, for instance, remains surprisingly complex. Mozilla points out that even after adjusting multiple settings, key elements of the operating system still open links in Edge, effectively bypassing the user's chosen browser.
Examples like Windows Search and Microsoft's productivity apps make that imbalance clear. Taskbar searches can still open in Edge by default, and links clicked in Outlook or Teams can also open in Edge, even when another browser is set as the default. Mozilla contends that these consistent redirects don't just inconvenience users; they distort competition in favor of Microsoft's own stack.
Because Windows continues to dominate the PC market, those design decisions have real commercial impact. The more Windows channels users into Edge, the less Firefox is used. For Mozilla, whose revenue comes primarily from search partnerships tied to browser activity, that means fewer searches and less funding to develop new features. It's an economic feedback loop that hits open-source developers harder than platform owners with deeper pockets.
Microsoft's Copilot rollout intensified that tension. The AI assistant was not just introduced aggressively; it showed up by default on many machines, pinned to the Windows taskbar, and, on some new laptops, mapped to a dedicated Copilot key. Mozilla argues that planned Copilot hooks in tools like File Explorer and system settings extend Microsoft's ecosystem into areas where browser rivals have little or no reach.
From Mozilla's point of view, these moves reveal a pattern: Microsoft embedding its own services so deeply into the OS that alternatives are effectively out of reach. Mozilla warns that if people rely more on built-in AI tools tied to Microsoft's ecosystem, they will spend less time in independent browsers like Firefox.
Mozilla frames its approach to AI differently. Mozilla said new AI features in Firefox are opt-in and can be turned off from a central settings panel – tools meant to empower users, not predetermine their experience.
external.title: Mozilla says Microsoft is using Copilot and Edge to tighten its grip on Windows

I don't make videos even though that was supposedly the future of writing ten years ago — I mean, I guess I do, but they're little banjo practice videos for my friends, not expressions of a desperate careerist impulse. I use social media with the attention-crazed dopesickness of an addict, not the care and forethought of a professional, as any cursory read of my posts will confirm. I'm a writer by trade — a creative, as you might say — and here you are, running a business you don't even understand into the ground, deskilling my trade and expecting a thank you when you explain how you're doing it. If I don't devalue myself the way you continually devalue me, you warn, I'll be left behind. Okay, says I! Quit making it my problem and go! The dust you leave me in will settle, and in the meantime, I will keep doing my work in much the way I've done it since I first became literate.

images.alt:
Andrej Karpathy
@karpathy •1d
Judging by my ti there is a growing gap in understanding of Al capability.

The first issue I think is around recency and tier of use. I think a lot of people tried the free tier of ChatGPT somewhere last year and allowed it to inform their views on Al a little too much. This is a group of reactions laughing at various quirks of the models, hallucinations, etc. Yes I also saw the viral videos of OpenAl's Advanced Voice mode fumbling simple queries like "should I drive or walk to the carwash". The thing is that these free and old/deprecated models don't reflect the capability in the latest round of state of the art agentic models of this year, especially OpenAl Codex and Claude Code.
But that brings me to the second issue. Even if people paid $200/month to use the state of the art models, a lot of the capabilities are relatively "peaky" in highly technical areas. Typical queries around search, writing, advice, etc. are "not* the domain that has made the most noticeable and dramatic strides in capability.
Partly, this is due to the technical details of reinforcement learning and its use of verifiable rewards. But partly, it's also because these use cases are not sufficiently prioritized by the companies in their hillclimbing because they don't lead to as much $$$ value. The goldmines are elsewhere, and the focus comes along.
So that brings me to the second group of people, who *both* 1) pay for and use the state of the art frontier agentic models (OpenAl Codex / Claude Code) do so professionally in technical domains like programming, math and research. This group ol is subiect to the hishest amount of "Al Psvchosis"
